mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE rises during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts reflect synchronized consumer demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines during periods of high inflation indicate supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Negative returns fail to materialize when CPIAUCSL exceeds 225 and INDPRO declines, or the signal reverses direction during supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week of the data release.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of months with payroll gains, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level below 0.25 percent indicates a zero lower bound constraint that forces aggressive quantitative easing, compressing term premiums and driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25, or the signal loses predictive power once inflation expectations exceed 3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures signals weak consumer demand despite aggregate growth, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative returns, or positive returns dominate when GDPC1 rises while RSAFS declines.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contractions during periods of stable monetary policy indicate genuine demand weakness rather than financial stress, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize when INDPRO falls during policy pauses, or the signal becomes positive during financial crisis episodes.
